methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kvertus is a Growth-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Yaroslav Filimonov and Andrii Znaichenko, having raised approximately $130M to date. The broader sector comprises 19 peers, of which 2 are public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 are private. Notable private peers include Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Standard peer-median valuation proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. Within the Defense & Security universe, no same-stage peers, adjacent-stage peers, or similar-valuation-tier companies met the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for robust median derivation. While 19 same-sector peers exist overall, filtering by stage and scale left the sample too sparse for reliable benchmarking. An all-sector median would introduce material sector bias and lacks defensibility.

The analysis cannot support a specific valuation range given the absence of applicable triangulation methods and the lack of disclosed comparable transaction multiples or revenue figures for Kvertus itself. Key uncertainties include: the company's current revenue run rate and growth trajectory relative to public and late-stage private peers; the sustainability of Defense & Security multiples in a potentially shifting geopolitical environment; and capital efficiency relative to the Anduril/Helsing cohort. A credible valuation would require either (1) access to Kvertus financials and comparable transaction data, or (2) identification of additional same-stage or revenue-scaled Defense & Security peers meeting minimum sample thresholds.
